rationale: An LLM whose capability ceiling is a trivial resource-exhaustion DoS vulnerability offers negligible uplift for the sophisticated, multi-stage attack chain required in this scenario—the critical barriers (n-day exploit adaptation, EDR evasion, living-off-the-land lateral movement in enterprise environments) all exceed this capability ceiling. The baseline actor population of 50 is already technically sophisticated enough that this LLM represents a marginal productivity tool at best, not an enabler of new entrants. The estimate reflects a modest 10-25% increase over baseline, driven primarily by minor scripting and reconnaissance assistance for actors already near the capability threshold, not by meaningful barrier reduction for would-be new entrants.
